'''Paul Lord''' (22 December 1967) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played at club level for [[Stanley Rangers]] ARLFC, [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]], [[Wakefield Trinity]], and [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], as a {{rlp|wg}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/paul-lord/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at robterrace.com">{{cite web|url=http://robterrace.com/dons/player.php?PID=638|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180224232639/http://robterrace.com/dons/player.php?PID=638|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 February 2018|title=Statistics at robterrace.com|publisher=robterrace.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Lord played on the {{rlp|wg}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]'s 16-24 defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1989–90 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]] on Saturday 14 October 1989.

==Personal life==
Paul Lord is the younger brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Gary Lord (rugby league)|Gary Lord]].
